purpose: Machine-readable MCP write-safety contract. High-risk workflow writes and destructive domain delete tools must require dry-run confirmation through one shared confirmation guard; low-level destructive tools must advertise destructive semantics and return receipts.
confirmationExemptDestructiveToolsNote: Destructive delete/remove tools intentionally NOT behind the dry_run->confirm guard; each entry needs a written reason in this note. This list is empty: every destructive domain delete/remove tool is guarded via confirmationGuardedDomainTools.
_destructiveSupersetNote: The manifest (docs/mcp-tool-manifest.json) reports 23 destructiveHint:true tools; this contract guards 17 (confirmationGuardedDomainTools). The 23 is a superset: 17 confirmation-guarded domain delete/remove tools + 5 highRiskWorkflowTools (clockify_invoice_client_work, clockify_record_expense, clockify_request_time_off, clockify_schedule_work, clockify_setup_webhook), which use the same shared guard via maybeConfirm rather than the domain confirmationGuardedDomainTools list + 1 idempotentWorkflowTool (clockify_demo_cleanup). scripts/check-mcp-write-safety.mjs enforces both directions: every destructive _delete/_remove domain tool is guarded-or-exempt, and every confirmationGuardedDomainTools entry is in the manifest destructive set. Update this note if the counts change.

contains: High-risk workflow writes require preview confirmation
confirmation token does not match this tool call
dry_run confirmation required before executing this workflow
preview step before any confirmed write

confirmationRequiredMarkers: dry_run confirmation required before executing this workflow
